jokes.text: Precisely, you bitch! I need to know precisely. 28 minutes! I'm sorry.
jokes.setup: Dennis asks for the time and Dee gives an approximate answer
jokes.punchline: Dennis explodes with an unnecessarily harsh insult over a minor imprecision, then Dee immediately complies
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Dennis's narcissistic control freak nature and Dee's submissive response to abuse. The escalation from 'precisely' to 'you bitch' is perfectly calibrated.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Precisely, you bitch! I need to know precisely.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: 28 minutes!

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I'm sorry.

jokes.text: I forgot about Obama!
jokes.setup: Mac realizes there's traffic due to Obama being in town
jokes.punchline: He acts like Obama's presence is some obscure fact he should have remembered rather than major news
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character moment showing Mac's self-centered worldview where a Presidential visit is just an inconvenience to his movie plans.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I forgot about Obama!

jokes.text: Plus, I hear the guy hangs dong, and I'm very interested in seeing that.
jokes.setup: Dee explains her interest in the action movie as a love story
jokes.punchline: She immediately reveals her actual motivation is seeing male nudity, stated matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection and character work. The casual delivery of crude motivation after the fake intellectual justification is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Plus, I hear the guy hangs dong, and I'm very interested in seeing that.

jokes.text: It's all these goddamn immigrants come in to see Obama. And none of them have any insurance. That's why they drive so slow.
jokes.setup: Traffic jam context
jokes.punchline: Charlie's bizarre racist logic connecting immigration, Obama, insurance, and driving speed
jokes.explanation: Dark character comedy showing Charlie's conspiracy-minded racism through completely illogical connections. Classic Sunny subversive humor.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: It's all these goddamn immigrants come in to see Obama. And none of them have any insurance. That's why they drive so slow.

jokes.text: Mac, that's because you always try and barge in so you can see the chicks naked. And then I have to start the process all over again.
jokes.setup: Mac criticizes Dennis for being slow with women
jokes.punchline: Dennis reveals Mac literally interrupts his sexual encounters to see naked women
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation revealing disturbing character dynamics. The matter-of-fact delivery of this insane behavior pattern is perfectly crafted.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Mac, that's because you always try and barge in so you can see the chicks naked. And then I have to start the process all over again.

jokes.text: I'm forging a connection with these women that will yield for them the most awakening and unforgettable sexual experience of their lives.
jokes.setup: Dennis defending his approach to women
jokes.punchline: Narcissistic delusion delivered with complete sincerity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis character moment - the grandiose language and complete lack of self-awareness is quintessential narcissistic delusion.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I'm forging a connection with these women that will yield for them the most awakening and unforgettable sexual experience of their lives.

jokes.text: Thundergun's not a verb. Stop using it as a verb.
jokes.setup: Gang keeps saying 'Thundergun it up'
jokes.punchline: Dennis's pedantic correction amid chaos
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy showing Dennis's need to correct others even in inappropriate moments. The grammar pedantry is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Thundergun's not a verb. Stop using it as a verb.

jokes.text: Well, I tell you one thing. I could walk faster than we're moving.
jokes.setup: Traffic jam frustration building
jokes.punchline: Frank's statement immediately leads to them actually walking
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for the escalation to walking. Functional but not particularly original or memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Well, I tell you one thing. I could walk faster than we're moving.

jokes.text: Thundergun leaves no man behind! We'll come back for you!
jokes.setup: They abandon Dennis to walk to the movie
jokes.punchline: They invoke the movie's motto while immediately contradicting it
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic setup for the running gag - they immediately abandon their friend while invoking the movie's code about loyalty.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Thundergun leaves no man behind! We'll come back for you!

jokes.text: Dude, hangs dong. I told them that! I told them! I don't want to miss that!
jokes.setup: Police officer confirms the movie is great
jokes.punchline: Frank's excited confirmation focuses solely on male nudity
jokes.explanation: Solid callback to the dong obsession, Frank's enthusiasm for this specific aspect is characteristically inappropriate.

dialogue.line: Dude, hangs dong.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I told them that! I told them! I don't want to miss that!

jokes.text: A cab? Dee, don't be a fool! We'll never get a cab out here. Plus, we'll just sit in traffic like everybody else.
jokes.setup: Dee suggests taking a cab
jokes.punchline: Frank's dismissive logic that ignores they're now walking
jokes.explanation: Basic character moment showing Frank's stubborn logic. Not particularly memorable but serves the plot.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: A cab? Dee, don't be a fool! We'll never get a cab out here. Plus, we'll just sit in traffic like everybody else.

jokes.text: We'll come back for you! You bastards!
jokes.setup: They abandon Frank at the trolley
jokes.punchline: Another false promise immediately followed by Frank's angry reaction
jokes.explanation: Continues the abandonment pattern, Frank's immediate curse undermines their promise. Good callback structure.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: You bastards!

jokes.text: What's with all the locks? I want you to feel safe.
jokes.setup: Dennis brings a woman to his apartment
jokes.punchline: Excessive locks presented as safety but clearly sinister
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark moment revealing Dennis's predatory nature. The false reassurance makes it more disturbing and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Alison
dialogue.line: What's with all the locks?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I want you to feel safe.

jokes.text: So your father took off when you were pretty young, huh? That must have been difficult.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to be charming
jokes.punchline: He's using daddy issues as a seduction technique
jokes.explanation: Perfectly creepy Dennis behavior - exploiting trauma for sexual gain

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: So your father took off when you were pretty young, huh? That must have been difficult.

jokes.text: Captain Tom turned out to be a goddamn junkie!
jokes.setup: Frank takes over a river boat tour
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of the real captain with extreme accusation
jokes.explanation: Frank's casual criminality and immediate character assassination of the real captain is perfectly absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Captain Tom turned out to be a goddamn junkie!

jokes.text: That's a big building. It's a brick building, red brick building. I don't know that the hell it is. It's a old building.
jokes.setup: Tourist asks what a building is during boat tour
jokes.punchline: Frank's completely useless 'tour guide' description
jokes.explanation: Frank's complete incompetence as a tour guide, stating obvious visual details while admitting ignorance.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: That's a big building. It's a brick building, red brick building. I don't know that the hell it is. It's a old building.

jokes.text: It's home to many weird fish-like creatures. And also the depository of all of the unsolved crimes and murders in Philadelphia.
jokes.setup: Frank attempts to describe the river
jokes.punchline: Escalates from mild weirdness to disturbing criminal implications
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from mundane to macabre. Frank casually suggesting the river is full of murder evidence is perfectly dark.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: It's home to many weird fish-like creatures. And also the depository of all of the unsolved crimes and murders in Philadelphia.

jokes.text: Plus, I hung dong on the trolley. You guys missed that.
jokes.setup: Charlie reports on their trolley success
jokes.punchline: He's proud of accidentally exposing himself
jokes.explanation: Callback to the dong obsession but with Charlie's characteristic cluelessness about appropriate behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Plus, I hung dong on the trolley. You guys missed that.

jokes.text: Looked like a button in a fur coat.
jokes.setup: Dee admits she saw Charlie's exposure
jokes.punchline: Devastating visual description
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual metaphor that's both specific and devastating. The imagery is perfectly crafted and highly quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Looked like a button in a fur coat.

jokes.text: It was more of a ding than a dong really, Mac.
jokes.setup: Previous dong discussion
jokes.punchline: Wordplay diminishing Charlie further
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that continues the emasculation. The ding/dong distinction is both crude and witty.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: It was more of a ding than a dong really, Mac.

jokes.text: He's scared he's going to be too fat to fit down that gutter. That is what it is.
jokes.setup: Dee refuses to go in the sewer
jokes.punchline: Charlie's blunt fat-shaming
jokes.explanation: Basic fat joke but fits Charlie's tactless character. Not particularly clever but serves the character dynamics.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: He's scared he's going to be too fat to fit down that gutter. That is what it is.

jokes.text: It turns out I-I am... I am too muscular, and I-I can't fit through. So my-my-my muscles stop me from gaining access.
jokes.setup: Mac gets stuck in the sewer grate
jokes.punchline: He blames his muscles instead of admitting he's fat
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac delusion - reframing obvious fatness as muscularity. The stammering delivery sells his desperation to maintain the lie.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: It turns out I-I am... I am too muscular, and I-I can't fit through. So my-my-my muscles stop me from gaining access.

jokes.text: That's what I told Dennis and Frank, and I was lying.
jokes.setup: Charlie promises to come back for Mac
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits he's lying about coming back
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - Charlie openly admits the pattern of abandonment and deception. The honesty about dishonesty is perfectly crafted.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: That's what I told Dennis and Frank, and I was lying.

jokes.text: Sixteen?! No get... what are you... get... Whoa, shit. That's not good. Why do I still have this tape?
jokes.setup: Dennis discovers his date is underage
jokes.punchline: His main concern is evidence, not the moral issue
jokes.explanation: Deeply dark character moment revealing Dennis's sociopathic priorities. The immediate shift to self-preservation is chillingly funny.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Sixteen?! No get... what are you... get... Whoa, shit. That's not good. Why do I still have this tape?

jokes.text: If a wall of water comes through, it's actually pretty sweet to be naked 'cause then you can hold your clothes up. You know, and then that piss and shit just kind of flows over you. It's more refreshing than you think.
jokes.setup: Charlie suggests getting naked in the sewer
jokes.punchline: His disgusting logic presented as reasonable advice
jokes.explanation: Classic Charlie logic - presenting revolting ideas as practical solutions with complete sincerity. The 'refreshing' description is perfectly absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: If a wall of water comes through, it's actually pretty sweet to be naked 'cause then you can hold your clothes up. You know, and then that piss and shit just kind of flows over you. It's more refreshing than you think.

jokes.text: I'll come back for you. Don't... don't you do that. Don't you... Charlie, get back here right now!
jokes.setup: Dee gets stuck in sewer
jokes.punchline: Charlie abandons her mid-promise, continuing the pattern
jokes.explanation: Continues the abandonment running gag effectively. Dee's desperation contrasts with Charlie's immediate departure.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I'll come back for you.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Don't... don't you do that. Don't you... Charlie, get back here right now!

jokes.text: I also invented Facebook with the Winklevoss twins. Really? Yeah, yeah. Zuckerberg didn't do shit.
jokes.setup: Dennis lies about owning auto repair shops
jokes.punchline: Escalates to claiming he invented Facebook
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Dennis's pathological lying. The Facebook claim is appropriately grandiose for his character.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I also invented Facebook with the Winklevoss twins.

dialogue.speaker: Elena
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah. Zuckerberg didn't do shit.

jokes.text: I seen it twice already. Oh, it's amazing.
jokes.setup: Elena joining them for the movie
jokes.punchline: She's already seen the ridiculous movie twice
jokes.explanation: Reinforces how universally loved this ridiculous movie is

dialogue.speaker: Elena
dialogue.line: I seen it twice already. Oh, it's amazing.

jokes.text: You gotta pay the troll toll to get into this boy's hole. I was saying 'soul.' He thought I was saying 'boy's hole.'
jokes.setup: Frank recounts Charlie's musical during boat tour
jokes.punchline: The inappropriate song and awkward clarification
jokes.explanation: Classic callback to previous episode with the awkward sexual implications. Frank's delivery makes it more disturbing.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: You gotta pay the troll toll to get into this boy's hole. I was saying 'soul.' He thought I was saying 'boy's hole.'

jokes.text: Frank's insane boat tour rambling about Charlie, waitress, crack, baby, troll song
jokes.setup: Frank giving boat tour
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate personal stories instead of tour information
jokes.explanation: Brilliant stream of consciousness referencing show history instead of giving tour

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Frank's insane boat tour rambling about Charlie, waitress, crack, baby, troll song

jokes.text: One of the things I like doing most is banging whores. I go out and bang a lot of whores.
jokes.setup: Frank continues his inappropriate boat tour
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of frequenting prostitutes to tourists
jokes.explanation: Frank's complete inappropriateness as tour guide continues. The matter-of-fact delivery sells the shock value.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: One of the things I like doing most is banging whores. I go out and bang a lot of whores.

jokes.text: One of the things I like doing most is banging whores. I, uh, I go out and bang a lot of whores.
jokes.setup: Frank continuing inappropriate boat tour
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions prostitution as hobby to tourists
jokes.explanation: Frank's complete inappropriateness as tour guide reaches peak

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: One of the things I like doing most is banging whores. I, uh, I go out and bang a lot of whores.

jokes.text: No hesitation. No surrender. No surren... Oh, shit. I surrender.
jokes.setup: Frank repeats the Thundergun motto while jumping
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts it when he hits the water
jokes.explanation: Perfect physical comedy timing. Frank's immediate surrender after invoking the 'no surrender' motto is well-executed irony.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: No hesitation. No surrender. No surren... Oh, shit. I surrender.

jokes.text: That's shit in my hair, that's why.
jokes.setup: Someone complains about Dee's smell in theater
jokes.punchline: Dee's matter-of-fact explanation
jokes.explanation: Blunt payoff to the sewer storyline. Dee's casual admission of her disgusting state gets the laugh through economy.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: That's shit in my hair, that's why.

jokes.text: Well, why don't you switch seats with us if it's bothering you so much. Just switch seats, you know what I mean?
jokes.setup: Other moviegoers complain about their talking
jokes.punchline: Their solution is to make others move rather than be quiet
jokes.explanation: Shows their selfishness and lack of consideration. The logic that others should accommodate them is appropriately narcissistic.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Well, why don't you switch seats with us if it's bothering you so much. Just switch seats, you know what I mean?

jokes.text: I'd like to report a bomb threat.
jokes.setup: Frank gets one phone call from jail
jokes.punchline: Uses it to call in fake bomb threat to delay movie
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Mac's earlier suggestion. Frank actually following through on the insane plan is peak character consistency and dark humor.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I'd like to report a bomb threat.

jokes.text: Goddamn it, Frank. He Thundergunned all of us. He Thundergunned the shit out of us.
jokes.setup: Bomb threat evacuates theater just as movie starts
jokes.punchline: Dennis realizes Frank out-Thundergunned them and uses it as a verb despite his earlier correction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback to Dennis's grammar correction - he now uses Thundergun as a verb himself. Perfect circular irony and character growth.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Goddamn it, Frank. He Thundergunned all of us. He Thundergunned the shit out of us.
